label: fixture
description: Real output of ConfigSchema.Build(), used by smoke.mjs so build() renders every section. Regenerate when config sections change: dump JsonSerializer.Serialize(ConfigSchema.Build(), ConfigSchema.Json) to this file. Drift only reduces coverage; it does not cause false failures.

properties.label: Credentials
properties.description: Optional credentials to connect to MQTT

properties.label: Username
properties.description: Username to log in as

properties.label: Password
properties.description: Password to login with

properties.label: ClientID
properties.description: The client-id used when connecting to MQTT.

properties.label: ParentTopic
properties.description: The parent topic for MQTT messages.

properties.label: Connection
properties.description: Connection details for MQTT Broker

properties.label: Connection Scheme
properties.description: How to reach the broker: mqtt (plain TCP), mqtts (TLS), ws (WebSocket) or wss (WebSocket over TLS). Leave unset to infer from the port.

properties.label: Host
properties.description: Hostname or IP to connect to.

properties.label: Port
properties.description: The port to connect to.

properties.label: Connection Timeout
properties.description: Default connection timeout.

properties.label: Validate Certificate
properties.description: Enables certificate validation

properties.label: KeepAlive
properties.description: The keepalive interval for the MQTT connection in seconds.

properties.label: Last Will / Availability
properties.description: Publish a Last-Will message and set an availability topic on entities, so Home Assistant marks them unavailable immediately when the bridge disconnects. When off, entities instead rely on HomeAssistant.SensorExpireAfterSeconds (expire_after) to go unavailable.

label: Pdus
description: PDU instances to bridge, keyed by instance name.

properties.label: Connection
properties.description: Connection details for PDU

properties.label: Host
properties.description: Hostname or IP to connect to.

properties.label: Port
properties.description: The port to connect to.

properties.label: Connection Timeout
properties.description: Default connection timeout.

properties.label: Connection Scheme
properties.description: Connection scheme used

properties.label: Validate Certificate
properties.description: Enables certificate validation

properties.label: Credentials
properties.description: PDU login used for write actions (outlet control). Can also be supplied via RPDU2MQTT_PDU_USERNAME / RPDU2MQTT_PDU_PASSWORD.

properties.label: Username
properties.description: Username to log in as

properties.label: Password
properties.description: Password to login with

properties.label: Poll Interval (seconds)
properties.description: How often (seconds) to poll the PDU and publish readings.

properties.label: Enable Write Actions
properties.description: Allow the bridge to make changes on the PDU (e.g. toggle outlets). When off, no switches or other write controls are exposed to Home Assistant. Requires PDU credentials.

properties.label: Remap Model column
properties.description: Replace each outlet/group's Model (shown in the Home Assistant device info) with contextual text (e.g. parent PDU + name) instead of the PDU's hardware model.

properties.label: Remap Manufacturer column
properties.description: Replace each entity's Manufacturer (shown in Home Assistant) with the entity type (Outlet, Group, etc.) instead of the hardware manufacturer.

properties.label: DiscoveryEnabled
properties.description: Indicates whether Home Assistant discovery is enabled.

properties.label: DiscoveryTopic
properties.description: The discovery topic for Home Assistant.

properties.label: DiscoveryInterval
properties.description: How often (seconds) to republish discovery. 0 = run once at startup until restarted.

properties.label: DiscoveryRetain
properties.description: Whether discovery messages are retained on the broker.

properties.label: SensorExpireAfterSeconds
properties.description: expire_after (seconds) applied to sensors; after this long without an update Home Assistant marks them unavailable.

properties.label: GroupMemberNameTemplate
properties.description: Name template for a group's mirrored member switches. Placeholders: {device}, {outlet}, {number}, {group}.

properties.label: GroupMemberObjectIdTemplate
properties.description: Stable entity/object_id template for a group's mirrored member switches. Placeholders: {serial}, {number}, {device}, {group}.

properties.label: EnergyDashboard
properties.description: Auto-configure Home Assistant's Energy Dashboard 'devices' (with upstream relationships) from the energy-flow hierarchy, via HA's WebSocket API.

properties.label: Enabled
properties.description: Sync the energy-flow hierarchy into HA's Energy Dashboard via its API. Requires Url + a long-lived access token.

properties.label: Url
properties.description: Home Assistant base URL, e.g. http://homeassistant.local:8123 .

properties.label: Token
properties.description: Home Assistant long-lived access token (or set RPDU2MQTT_HASS_TOKEN).

properties.label: EnergyMeasurementType
properties.description: Measurement type holding each entity's cumulative energy (kWh) — used to find the Energy Dashboard stat for each tier.

properties.label: PDU
properties.description: Allows overriding values for the rPDU2MQTT.

properties.label: ID
properties.description: Overridden ID

properties.label: Name
properties.description: Overridden Name

properties.label: Enabled
properties.description: Is this entity enabled?

properties.label: Make
properties.description: Manufacturer shown in Home Assistant (e.g. 'Dell'). Applies to devices, outlets and groups.

properties.label: Model
properties.description: Model shown in Home Assistant (e.g. 'PowerEdge R730xd'). Applies to devices, outlets and groups.

properties.label: Devices
properties.description: Allows overriding configuration for individual devices.

properties.label: Outlets
properties.description: Allows overriding values for individual outlets.

properties.label: ID
properties.description: Overridden ID

properties.label: Name
properties.description: Overridden Name

properties.label: Enabled
properties.description: Is this entity enabled?

properties.label: Make
properties.description: Manufacturer shown in Home Assistant (e.g. 'Dell'). Applies to devices, outlets and groups.

properties.label: Model
properties.description: Model shown in Home Assistant (e.g. 'PowerEdge R730xd'). Applies to devices, outlets and groups.

properties.label: ID
properties.description: Overridden ID

properties.label: Name
properties.description: Overridden Name

properties.label: Enabled
properties.description: Is this entity enabled?

properties.label: Make
properties.description: Manufacturer shown in Home Assistant (e.g. 'Dell'). Applies to devices, outlets and groups.

properties.label: Model
properties.description: Model shown in Home Assistant (e.g. 'PowerEdge R730xd'). Applies to devices, outlets and groups.

properties.label: Measurements
properties.description: Allows overriding individual measurements

properties.label: ID
properties.description: Overridden ID

properties.label: Name
properties.description: Overridden Name

properties.label: Enabled
properties.description: Is this entity enabled?

properties.label: Make
properties.description: Manufacturer shown in Home Assistant (e.g. 'Dell'). Applies to devices, outlets and groups.

properties.label: Model
properties.description: Model shown in Home Assistant (e.g. 'PowerEdge R730xd'). Applies to devices, outlets and groups.

properties.label: OneviewGroups
properties.description: Overrides specific to Oneview groups

properties.label: Measurements
properties.description: Allows overriding individual measurements for groups

properties.label: ID
properties.description: Overridden ID

properties.label: Name
properties.description: Overridden Name

properties.label: Enabled
properties.description: Is this entity enabled?

properties.label: Make
properties.description: Manufacturer shown in Home Assistant (e.g. 'Dell'). Applies to devices, outlets and groups.

properties.label: Model
properties.description: Model shown in Home Assistant (e.g. 'PowerEdge R730xd'). Applies to devices, outlets and groups.

properties.label: Overrides
properties.description: Allows overriding group ID, Name, and enabled state.

properties.label: ID
properties.description: Overridden ID

properties.label: Name
properties.description: Overridden Name

properties.label: Enabled
properties.description: Is this entity enabled?

properties.label: Make
properties.description: Manufacturer shown in Home Assistant (e.g. 'Dell'). Applies to devices, outlets and groups.

properties.label: Model
properties.description: Model shown in Home Assistant (e.g. 'PowerEdge R730xd'). Applies to devices, outlets and groups.

properties.label: Publish to MQTT
properties.description: Actually publish messages to the broker. Turn off to dry-run the whole pipeline without sending anything.

properties.label: Print Discovery Payloads
properties.description: Log the Home Assistant discovery JSON to the console (verbose; for troubleshooting discovery).

properties.label: Exporter
properties.description: Expose a /metrics endpoint for Prometheus to scrape.

properties.label: Port
properties.description: Port the /metrics endpoint listens on (Exporter).

properties.label: MetricNameTemplate
properties.description: Template for Prometheus metric names. Placeholders: {type}, {device}, {source} (a.k.a. {outlet}), {units}. e.g. 'rpdu2mqtt_{type}' -> rpdu2mqtt_realpower; 'pdu_{device}_{type}' -> pdu_rack_pdu_1_realpower. (device/source/units are also emitted as labels.)

properties.label: Labels
properties.description: Labels attached to every exported metric. Available: device, source, name, number, type, units, instance (PDU instance key), hierarchy (the energy-flow tier feeding it). Changing this changes every metric's label set.

properties.label: Pushgateway
properties.description: Push metrics to a Prometheus Pushgateway.

properties.label: Enabled
properties.description: Push metrics to a Pushgateway.

properties.label: Url
properties.description: Pushgateway endpoint, e.g. http://pushgateway:9091/metrics.

properties.label: Job
properties.description: The 'job' label applied to pushed metrics.

properties.label: IntervalSeconds
properties.description: How often to push, in seconds. Falls back to the PDU poll interval when 0.

properties.label: Enabled
properties.description: Push measurements to an EmonCMS server.

properties.label: Transport
properties.description: How to deliver measurements: Http (input/post API) or Mqtt (publish to EmonCMS's MQTT input on the same broker).

properties.label: Url
properties.description: Base URL of the EmonCMS server, e.g. http://emoncms.example.com. (Http transport.)

properties.label: ApiKey
properties.description: EmonCMS write API key (or set RPDU2MQTT_EMONCMS_APIKEY). (Http transport.)

properties.label: Node
properties.description: EmonCMS input node name.

properties.label: Path
properties.description: API path (relative to Url) that measurements are posted to. (Http transport.)

properties.label: InputNameTemplate
properties.description: Template for EmonCMS input keys. Placeholders: {device}, {source} (object-id form), {name} (formatted display name), {number} (outlet number), {type}, {units}. e.g. '{device}_{source}_{type}' -> rack_pdu_1_dell_md1200_realpower. Leave blank to use the full raw identifier.

properties.label: MqttBaseTopic
properties.description: Base MQTT topic for EmonCMS's MQTT input (the {base} placeholder of MqttTopicTemplate). (Mqtt transport.)

properties.label: MqttTopicTemplate
properties.description: Template for the EmonCMS MQTT topic each JSON payload is published to. Placeholders: {base} (MqttBaseTopic), {node}, {device} (the PDU's name). Including {device} splits the export so each PDU publishes to its own topic instead of one combined payload, e.g. '{base}/{node}/{device}'. (Mqtt transport.)

properties.label: Feeds
properties.description: Automatically create and maintain EmonCMS feeds from the exported inputs. (Http transport; needs a read/write API key.)

properties.label: AutoConfigure
properties.description: Create and maintain EmonCMS feeds from the exported inputs (takes effect without a restart).

properties.label: Types
properties.description: The measurement types to build feeds for, each with its own engine, interval and processing.

properties.label: Type
properties.description: The measurement type this applies to (raw PDU type name).

properties.label: Engine
properties.description: Feed storage engine for this type. Blank inherits Feeds.Engine. PHPFina = fixed-interval, PHPTimeSeries = variable, MySQL = MySQL storage.

properties.label: IntervalSeconds
properties.description: Sample interval in seconds for this type's feed. Blank inherits Feeds.IntervalSeconds.

properties.label: Daily
properties.description: Also create a daily kWh/d feed (an extra processlist step: kWh→kWh/d). Typically only for the energy type.

properties.label: DailyIntervalSeconds
properties.description: Interval (seconds) for the daily kWh/d feed. Should be a day (86400), not the base interval.

properties.label: Engine
properties.description: Default feed storage engine, for types that don't set their own. PHPFina = fixed-interval time series, PHPTimeSeries = variable interval, MySQL = MySQL storage (no phpfina files).

properties.label: IntervalSeconds
properties.description: Default sample interval in seconds for a fixed-interval feed, for types that don't set their own.

properties.label: IdempotentNames
properties.description: Name storage feeds idempotently from stable ids (device/source/type) so they DON'T change when a source is renamed. Turn off to name them from the display name instead (renaming a source renames its feed).

properties.label: Tag
properties.description: The EmonCMS tag (group) new feeds are filed under. Blank uses the input node name.

properties.label: StorageNameTemplate
properties.description: Template for the idempotent storage-feed name. Use only stable placeholders ({device}, {source}, {type}, {number}) so it never changes on a rename. Placeholders: {device}, {source}, {name}, {number}, {type}, {units}.

properties.label: Virtual
properties.description: Optionally create friendly-named virtual feeds that source from the stable storage feeds — so dashboards get nice names while the underlying feeds stay idempotent.

properties.label: Enabled
properties.description: Create a friendly-named virtual feed for each storage feed, sourced from it (source_feed process).

properties.label: NameTemplate
properties.description: Template for the friendly virtual-feed name. {name} is the source's display name, so these can change freely without touching the stable storage feeds. Placeholders: {device}, {source}, {name}, {number}, {type}, {units}.

properties.label: Tag
properties.description: The EmonCMS tag (group/node) virtual feeds are filed under — set this to keep the friendly virtual feeds separate from the storage feeds. Blank uses the main Feeds tag.

properties.label: Processes
properties.description: EmonCMS process ids used in the generated processlists. Match these to your EmonCMS (Inputs → process dropdown).

properties.label: LogToFeed
properties.description: Process id_num for 'Log to feed' (EmonCMS stores processlists as id_num:feedid; default 1).

properties.label: KwhToKwhd
properties.description: Process id_num for 'kWh to kWh/d', used for daily energy feeds (default 23).

properties.label: SourceFeed
properties.description: Process id_num for 'Source feed', used for virtual feeds (default 53).

properties.label: Enabled
properties.description: Enable the embedded configuration web GUI.

properties.label: Authentication
properties.description: How users authenticate to the GUI: Basic (username/password), Oidc (SSO), or None (no login).

properties.label: Port
properties.description: Port the configuration GUI listens on.

properties.label: Username
properties.description: Username required to access the GUI (HTTP Basic auth).

properties.label: Password
properties.description: Password required to access the GUI (HTTP Basic auth). Required unless Oidc is enabled.

properties.label: Oidc
properties.description: OpenID Connect (SSO) settings (used when AuthType is Oidc).

properties.label: Authority
properties.description: OIDC authority / issuer URL (e.g. https://keycloak.example.com/realms/home).

properties.label: ClientId
properties.description: OIDC client ID registered with your identity provider.

properties.label: ClientSecret
properties.description: OIDC client secret. Prefer the RPDU2MQTT_OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET env var (or *_FILE secret).

properties.label: Scopes
properties.description: Space-separated scopes to request.

properties.label: CallbackPath
properties.description: Redirect/callback path registered with the identity provider.

properties.label: Enabled
properties.description: Expose HTTP health-check endpoints: /healthz (liveness) and /readyz (readiness).

properties.label: Port
properties.description: Port the health-check endpoints listen on.

properties.label: Enabled
properties.description: Expose a read-only REST API (/api/v1/*) with OpenAPI + Scalar docs on its own port. Place it on a trusted network — it is unauthenticated, like the health endpoints.

properties.label: Port
properties.description: Port the REST API + docs listen on.

properties.label: ApiKey
properties.description: Optional API key enabling the write/control endpoints (or set RPDU2MQTT_API_KEY). When unset, the API is read-only; when set, control requests must send a matching 'X-Api-Key' header. Reads stay open (trusted-network).

label: EnergyFlow
description: Virtual upstream nodes (breakers, transfer switches, a “Total”) and their feeder wiring for the energy-flow hierarchy. Edited visually on the Flow tab.

properties.label: Nodes
properties.description: Custom upstream flow nodes (breakers, transfer-switch outputs, Total, ...), keyed by a stable id.

properties.label: Id
properties.description: Stable unique id used to wire parents/children.

properties.label: Label
properties.description: Human-readable label shown in the flow diagram.

properties.label: Value
properties.description: Optional directly-known value for a leaf node (used until a live sensor is bound).

properties.label: Links
properties.description: Directed energy-flow links (From feeds To). Allows multiple feeders per node and producer inputs.

properties.label: From
properties.description: Source node id — energy flows out of here.

properties.label: To
properties.description: Target node id — energy flows into here.

properties.label: Parents
properties.description: Legacy single-feeder map (child id -> parent id). Prefer Links; still honored for back-compat.

properties.label: MqttExport
properties.description: Publish each energy-hierarchy tier's rolled-up value to MQTT every poll.

properties.label: MqttTopicTemplate
properties.description: Template for each tier's MQTT topic. Placeholders: {parent} (MQTT parent topic), {id}, {label}, {kind}, {metric}, {units}. e.g. '{parent}/energyflow/{id}'.
